title: Purely Roasted Peruvian Pineapple Tiradito
description: A roasted and tender Peruvian dish featuring pineapple and tempering chocolate, low in sodium and served as a tiradito. This dish is a comforting and unique dessert option.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together coconut milk, lime juice, ginger, cinnamon, and salt.
Add the diced pineapple to the bowl and toss to coat with the coconut milk mixture.
Spread the pineapple mixture on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Roast the pineapple in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until tender and lightly caramelized.
Melt the dark chocolate in a double boiler or in the microwave in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval.
Temper the melted chocolate by cooling it to around 82 degrees Fahrenheit.
To assemble the tiradito, place a portion of the roasted pineapple on a plate and drizzle with the tempered chocolate.
Sprinkle a pinch of cinnamon and a few drops of vanilla extract over the top of the pineapple and chocolate.
